pooled_verdict.core_verdict: no reliable positive-gain evidence under speaker-grouped evaluation
pooled_verdict.equivalence_verdict: bounded below SESOI (±0.05 absolute accuracy): the delta CI [-0.0305, +0.0474] lies entirely within the pre-declared smallest effect size of interest -- practically equivalent to no effect (TOST-style).

verdict.core_verdict: no reliable positive-gain evidence under speaker-grouped evaluation
verdict.equivalence_verdict: equivalence inconclusive: the delta CI [-0.0123, +0.0750] extends beyond the pre-declared ±0.05 SESOI, so neither a reliable gain nor a bounded-null claim is supported by this evidence alone.

verdict.core_verdict: no reliable positive-gain evidence under speaker-grouped evaluation
verdict.equivalence_verdict: equivalence inconclusive: the delta CI [-0.0732, +0.0854] extends beyond the pre-declared ±0.05 SESOI, so neither a reliable gain nor a bounded-null claim is supported by this evidence alone.

verdict.core_verdict: reliable negative-gain evidence under speaker-grouped evaluation (95% cluster-bootstrap CI [-0.0366, -0.0366] excludes 0)
verdict.equivalence_verdict: bounded below SESOI (±0.05 absolute accuracy): the delta CI [-0.0366, -0.0366] lies entirely within the pre-declared smallest effect size of interest -- practically equivalent to no effect (TOST-style).

sesoi.note: PRE-DECLARED smallest effect size of interest (absolute accuracy), fixed in this script's SESOI constant before the fold analysis ran; TOST-style equivalence check per ticket #34 requirement 4 / wiki 2026-07-11-response-v2-erratum-and-forensic-reply.md RR-005/E2 (Lakens 2017 convention referenced there).

config.method: GroupKFold(n_splits, shuffle=True, random_state=fold_seed) over CREMA-D actor ID; per-fold layer dev-selected on an inner speaker-grouped split of the fold's train speakers only; cluster(=speaker) bootstrap on the per-clip (sel - base) delta, per-fold and pooled across all folds.
